comments.comment:
You've made some great strides in your understanding of the Monroe Doctrine! I really appreciated how you summarized its main message with a relatable tone, saying it told Europeans to 'Get lost.' This shows you're grasping the essence of the doctrine and its implications for U.S. foreign policy. Your engagement with the material is clear, and it's great to see your effort reflected in your responses.

However, I noticed that some of your answers could benefit from a bit more detail. For instance, in your first response, you mentioned that European nations didn't believe the U.S. was strong enough, but it would be helpful to explain why they thought that. Adding context can really strengthen your points and show deeper critical thinking.

Also, while your writing is getting clearer, try to keep your phrasing a bit more formal in academic settings. Phrases like 'sorta chilling' can detract from the seriousness of your analysis. Remember, you're doing a great job overall, and just a few tweaks can elevate your work even more!

Keep up the fantastic effort! Your consistent improvement in content accuracy and completeness is commendable. I look forward to seeing how you continue to develop your writing skills in future assignments.
